patches.hypothesis: The patch was overfitting due to hardcoded values and a lack of edge case handling. I refined the patch to use a more robust algorithm and added proper null checks and boundary conditions.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug was caused by trying to access an index in the `values` array that is out of bounds, because the size of the `values` array is not the same as the size of the `mapping` map.

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by trying to access an index in the values array that is out of bounds. The fix involves checking if the index is within the bounds of the array before attempting to access it.
The bug is caused by the array index out of bounds exception when the record has fewer columns than the mapping.
The bug is caused by trying to access an index in the `values` array that is out of bounds, which is caused by a short record not having enough values to fill all the expected columns.
